# Re-adding an already-tracked secret without --group is a no-op: it must not
# change the secret's access groups, emit a change_access entry, or rewrite the
# entry's access list in sesam.yml.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# First add with an explicit group.
exec sesam add some_file.txt --group dev
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"secret.add"'
exec sesam ls
stdout 'dev'

# Re-add the same path WITHOUT a group => no change, no change_access entry.
exec sesam add some_file.txt
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
! stdout '"secret.change_access"'

# The group is preserved and the access list was not cleared to `access: []`.
exec sesam ls
stdout 'dev'
grep 'dev' sesam.yml
! grep 'access: \[\]' sesam.yml
exec sesam verify

-- some_file.txt --
Some content
